channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17925] sanity-flr test_0b: lfs mirror create: cannot create composite file: Numerical result out of range
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for sarah <sarah@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/79949d91-729b-49a4-a720-86d32692512f

test_0b failed with the following error:

lfs mirror create: cannot create composite file '/mnt/lustre/d0b.sanity-flr/f0b.sanity-flr': Numerical result out of range
 sanity-flr test_0b: @@@@@@ FAIL: create mirrored file /mnt/lustre/d0b.sanity-flr/f0b.sanity-flr failed

Test session details:
clients: 2.15.63.161
servers: 2.14.0.21

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

OSS dmesg shows

[44280.325037] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity-flr test 0b: lfs mirror create plain layout mirrors ========================================================== 18:43:57 (1717181037)
onyx-40vm6: Pool lustre.archive created
CMD: onyx-40vm6 lctl get_param -n lod.lustre-MDT0000-mdtlov.pools.archive 				2>/dev/null || echo foo
CMD: onyx-40vm1.onyx.whamcloud.com lctl get_param -n lov.lustre-*.pools.archive 		2>/dev/null || echo foo
lfs mirror create: cannot create composite file '/mnt/lustre/d0b.sanity-flr/f0b.sanity-flr': Numerical result out of range
 sanity-flr test_0b: @@@@@@ FAIL: create mirrored file /mnt/lustre/d0b.sanity-flr/f0b.sanity-flr failed

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-flr test_0b - create mirrored file /mnt/lustre/d0b.sanity-flr/f0b.sanity-flr failed
item.summary: sanity-flr test_0b: lfs mirror create: cannot create composite file: Numerical result out of range

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Andreas Dilger <adilger@thelustrecollective.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63797
Subject: LU-17925 llapi: quiet -Wsign-conversion warnings
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 928aa3c88f7e248b5ddc4533df17639d6e63037c
Merged for 2.16

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55548/
Subject: LU-17925 utils: fix 'lfs setstripe -c -1' interop
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 73fd18caa6f7951e88aedd17cd87689f74921a73

"Rajeev Mishra <rajeevm@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55548
Subject: LU-17925 utils: test_0b fails in interop environment
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 1801a522e3dd9c8513918d02c398a14853186dc9

rajeevm ,

Please try this in your commit message:

Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-flr env=ONLY=0b serverjob=lustre-b2_14 serverbuildno=8 serverdistro=el8.5

HTH.

for me test works with fix ( different version on client and server )...

[root@lustre-client tests]# lctl --version

lctl 2.15.63

 

server

[root@test1-rocky8 rocky]# lctl --version

lctl 2.15.64

seems to work fine

it has 3 0ST

lfs setstripe -C -1 /mnt/lustre/rajeev

[root@lustre-client tests]# lfs getstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev

/mnt/lustre/rajeev

lmm_stripe_count:  3

lmm_stripe_size:   4194304

lmm_pattern:       raid0

lmm_layout_gen:    0

lmm_stripe_offset: 0

obdidx objid objid group

    0             2           0x2   0x240000400

    1             2           0x2   0x280000400

    2             2           0x2   0x2c0000400

 

but client does not have 2.15.63.161 (mine is 2.15.63). This is with the fix

Thanks, Andreas. I will add the test parameters as suggested in the commit message and test it locally first. I am still searching for ways to obtain the exact client version.

The new fix is working between same version but still need to test interop.
For the client version 2.15.63.161, I didn't see a tag. Do you have any information on how I can obtain the code for this version? Specifically, do you have the tag or commit hash details?"

rajeevm you can submit an interop test request on your patch with the following line in the commit message, right above your Signed-off-by: line:

Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-flr serverversion=2.14

This should be done to confirm that the patch is fixing the interop problem.
my fix is ready, trying to setup client will lower build to check inter op
I will try to get fix asap.

rajeevm ,

Test session details:
clients: 2.15.63.161
servers: 2.14.0.21

This is an interop testing issue. The server should be 2.14. Could you please try again?

Thanks.

That’s an interesting point. The stripe count will now come as 0xffff since the older version won't send 0xffd. I’ll update the logic to start from 0xffff instead of 0xffd. This means that reverse counting will start from 0xffff,  rather than 0xfffd.

I’ll set up a three-node system with different versions to test this change. Good catch—I didn’t consider that the code needs to be compatible with older version -1 should work on old version as well. Thanks for pointing that out. I’ll recreate the setup and fix it.

Update on Test 0b Execution

I am running the test_0b on a single-node system with 5 OSTs. The test passes as shown below. Here are the detailed steps and commands executed:

MD: test1-single-node lctl pool_remove lustre.test_0b lustre-OST0001_UUID

OST lustre-OST0001_UUID removed from pool lustre.test_0b

CMD: test1-single-node lctl pool_remove lustre.test_0b lustre-OST0002_UUID

OST lustre-OST0002_UUID removed from pool lustre.test_0b

CMD: test1-single-node lctl pool_remove lustre.test_0b lustre-OST0003_UUID

OST lustre-OST0003_UUID removed from pool lustre.test_0b

CMD: test1-single-node lctl pool_remove lustre.test_0b lustre-OST0004_UUID

OST lustre-OST0004_UUID removed from pool lustre.test_0b

CMD: test1-single-node lctl pool_list lustre.test_0b | wc -l

CMD: test1-single-node lctl pool_destroy lustre.test_0b

Pool lustre.test_0b destroyed

CMD: test1-single-node lctl get_param -n lod.lustre-MDT0000-mdtlov.pools.test_0b 2>/dev/null || echo foo

CMD: test1-single-node lctl get_param n lov.lustre*.pools.test_0b 2>/dev/null || echo foo

PASS 0b (19s)

 

Clarification on Stripe Count Logic

The following is the logic for the most confusing part of the code regarding stripe count adjustments:

 
if (stripe_count > lov_desc->ld_active_tgt_count) {
    if (overstriping) {
        if (stripe_count >= LOV_ALL_STRIPES_MIN && stripe_count <= LOV_ALL_STRIPES_MAX)

{             stripe_count = ((stripe_count - LOV_ALL_STRIPES_MIN) + 1) * lov_desc->ld_active_tgt_count;         }

    }
}
 
Explanation:

    When -C or -c -1 is passed, the stripe count will be 0xffdf, which results in:
(0xffdf - 0xffdf + 1) * ost_count

lfs setstripe -C -1 /mnt/lustre/rajeev

[root@test1-single-node tests]# lfs getstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev

/mnt/lustre/rajeev

lmm_stripe_count:  5

lmm_stripe_size:   4194304

lmm_pattern:       raid0

lmm_layout_gen:    0

lmm_stripe_offset: 2

obdidx objid objid group

    2             19         0x13   0x2c0000400

    3             20         0x14   0x300000400

    4             18         0x12   0x340000400

    0             18         0x12   0x280000400

    1             18         0x12   0x240000400

When -C -2 is passed, the stripe count will be 0xffe0, which results in:

(0xffe0 - 0xffdf + 1) * ost_count

lfs setstripe -C -2 /mnt/lustre/rajeev

[root@test1-single-node tests]# lfs getstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev

/mnt/lustre/rajeev

lmm_stripe_count:  10

lmm_stripe_size:   4194304

lmm_pattern:       raid0,overstriped

lmm_layout_gen:    0

lmm_stripe_offset: 3

obdidx objid objid group

    3             21         0x15   0x300000400

    4             19         0x13   0x340000400

    0             19         0x13   0x280000400

    1             19         0x13   0x240000400

    2             20         0x14   0x2c0000400

    3             22         0x16   0x300000400

    4             20         0x14   0x340000400

    0             20         0x14   0x280000400

    1             20         0x14   0x240000400

    2             21         0x15   0x2c0000400

 

Please let me know if I missed any thing. I have shown simple test on the strip count also above. I need to understand what is failing to make it work
Analyzing the issue

aboyko commented in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/54192 "LU-16938 utils: setstripe overstripe multiple OST count"

			if (stripe_count >= LOV_ALL_STRIPES_MIN &&
			    stripe_count <= LOV_ALL_STRIPES_MAX) {
				stripe_count =  ((stripe_count - LOV_ALL_STRIPES_MIN) + 1) *
					lov_desc->ld_active_tgt_count;

I calculated wrong value here, maybe I'm missing something, could you please explain?
"Using "-C -1" will create one stripe on all (available) OSTs,"

if function takes as argument stripe_count = 0xffff (-1) which means only one stripe for all targets. At this calculation we get (0xffff-0xffdf+1)=33 * (target counts) -> looks like it is not what we expected?
1*active vs 33*active

It looks like this calculation is wrong and should be something like:

        stripe_count = (LOV_ALL_STRIPES_MAX - stripe_count + 1) *

so that it is using the negative of the specified stripe count.
cc paf and rajeevm who may have an interest/opinion in this interop failure...

Is this only failing in interop testing?

Yes, so far it is only failing in interop testing.

Definitely the "-c -2" functional testing should be skipped during interop testing, as this will not work with older MDS nodes. However, sanity-flr test_0b was not modified by the referenced patch (only test_0c), so it may be a real bug?

I'm investigating.

Test session details:
clients: 2.15.63.161
servers: 2.14.0.21

Is this only failing in interop testing? Definitely the "-c -2" functional testing should be skipped during interop testing, as this will not work with older MDS nodes. However, sanity-flr test_0b was not modified by the referenced patch (only test_0c), so it may be a real bug?

This issue was caused by "LU-16938 utils: setstripe overstripe multiple OST count https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54192/ ".

I will see how to fix it.

"Emoly Liu <emoly@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55473
Subject: LU-17925 lfs: debug why sanity-flr.sh test_0b failed
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 8052b64bea86ce78c2803a74f504e6aea3d40de5

The Maloo searching results showed this failure only happened after 2024-05-28. So, the patches between 2.15.63.48 and 2.15.63.91 should be related to this issue, including

LU-17783 statahead: disable batch statahead for old server (details / gitweb)
LU-17525 tests: fix sanity hash 2.15 interop (details / gitweb)
LU-13802 llite: add read & write switch thresholds (details / gitweb)
LU-13802 llite: add hybrid IO switch proc stats (details / gitweb)
LU-16938 utils: setstripe overstripe multiple OST count (details / gitweb)
LU-17851 ldiskfs: restart long fallocate tx (details / gitweb)
LU-6142 socklnd: SPDX for sockets LND (details / gitweb)
LU-17865 osc: fiemap deadlock fix (details / gitweb)
LU-11085 ldlm: move interval_insert call from ldlm_lock to ldlm_extent (details / gitweb)
LU-11085 ldlm: convert ldlm extent locks to linux extent-tree (details / gitweb)
LU-16350 ldiskfs: Server support for LTS linux v6.1 (details / gitweb)
LU-16819 build: use mofed path based on target kernel (details / gitweb)

I will look into it.

Emoly

Could you please investigate?

Thanks

Peter

This had one failure due to a timeout, which looks like it was caused by the LU-17197 RPC issue.

However, it looks like there are a lot more test failures during interop testing with 2.14.0 servers, so this should be investigated and skipped if it is caused by a known issue (or fixed if it is a real interop issue).
